questions.question: Describe the difference between the partial autocorrelation function and the autocorrelation function. Provide an example to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: Autocorrelation function is related to the correlation between stationary series at different lags, while partial autocorrelation function represents the correlation between series at different lags after removing the effects of intermediate lags.

questions.question: What is the significance of the spectral density plot in time series analysis? Explain with the help of a suitable example.
questions.hint: Spectral density plot is used to identify the frequencies present in the time series. It can help in identifying periodic patterns and in selecting the most appropriate model for the time series data.

questions.question: Write down the mathematical representation of the autocorrelation function of an ARMA process. Explain the meaning of each parameter in the representation.
questions.hint: If Xn is the ARMA process, then its autocorrelation function is given by: ρ(h) = {φ1 z(h-1) + ... + φp z(h-p) + θ1 ε(h-1) + ... + θq ε(h-q)} / (1 - z^(-1)φ1 - z^(-2)φ2 - ... - z^(-p)φp - z^(-q+1)θ1 - z^(-q+2)θ2 - ... - z^(-q)θq).
